title: fix(image): point Klein at new RunPod pod after host outage
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Old Klein pod `lqh6weiexk4sth` became unreachable after the RunPod balance-reclaim outage (no public SSH; relay non-functional).
- Recreated Klein on a **secure-cloud A5000** (`yd0mjovg0nx5pc`) — secure cloud gives reliable public SSH and survives balance dips, unlike community pods.
- Updates `KLEIN_URL` secret + hardcoded fallback in `fluxKleinModel.ts` to the new pod.

## Verification
- New pod `/health`: `{"status":"ok","model":"black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-4B"}`
- Direct `/generate`: HTTP 200, 4.6s, valid image
- Prod e2e currently 502 (deployed worker still has old URL) → fixed by this deploy

Klein was failing ~65% 5xx on production traffic. Restores it.

polly

gist.headline: Klein gets a new home
gist.blurb: Our Klein image model got a shiny new, ultra-reliable server after a host outage. It's back to generating your pixels smoothly.
gist.summary: Restored the FLUX.2-klein-4B image model by migrating it to a new secure-cloud A5000 RunPod instance.
gist.impact: Users will no longer experience 5xx errors when requesting the Klein model, with generation reliability significantly improved.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot carefully moving a glowing 'Klein' core into a sturdy, brand new retro server rack.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot carefully placing a glowing blue core into a sturdy new retro server rack. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
